jdBreakdown.roleSummary: Quota-carrying enterprise cloud sales role responsible for executive relationships, Azure consumption growth, partner co-selling, large migration and modernization deals, forecasting, negotiation, and deal closure.
mustHaveRequirements: 7+ years of technology sales or account management experience
7+ years leading large cloud engagements for enterprise customers
Quota, pipeline, forecasting, negotiation, and deal-closing ownership
Leadership of virtual sales, technical, services, licensing, and legal teams
Enterprise cloud migration or application modernization experience
strongAdvantageRequirements: Azure or public cloud platform fluency
AI and digital transformation customer advisory
niceToHaveRequirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, IT, Business Administration, or related field
Security, GenAI, and analytics solution knowledge
Three days per week in office
coreResponsibilities: Create executive buy-in for Microsoft solution value
Jointly sell with partners and ISVs
Build business cases and customer proposals
Drive Azure consumption or sales quota
Feed customer insights into go-to-market and product strategy

technical: Enterprise software and datacenter infrastructure knowledge
AI, analytics, and security solution fluency

senioritySignals: 7+ years technology sales or account management
Ownership of large enterprise cloud engagements
Virtual-team leadership across technical and commercial functions
hiddenHiringPriorities: Proof of quota attainment and cloud-consumption growth
Ability to access and influence C-level or executive decision makers
Ability to turn technical capabilities into measurable business cases
Reliable forecast discipline and deal inspection
Ability to remove commercial, technical, licensing, and legal blockers

positioning.primaryHiringProblem: Microsoft needs an experienced quota-carrying enterprise cloud seller who can grow Azure consumption through executive selling and large deal closure.
positioning.managerHireReason: Hire someone who already owns enterprise sales cycles, forecasts, partners, and multi-million-dollar cloud engagements.
positioning.toolApplicationAngle: The candidate can translate business workflows into Microsoft automation, AI, analytics, and governance solutions, but the JD requires commercial ownership and cloud-sales depth that are not evidenced.
positioningRationale: The role is fundamentally enterprise solution sales, not technical solution delivery or business automation.
Candidate evidence strongly supports Microsoft ecosystem workflow automation, AI adoption, analytics, governance, and stakeholder coordination.
No grounded evidence supports quota ownership, Azure consumption targets, executive selling, large cloud migrations, partner co-selling, or multi-million-dollar deal closure.
Technical cloud knowledge alone is explicitly insufficient for this role.
positioning.safestPositioning: Do not position as a qualified Azure Solution Specialist. If applying, present as a business-to-technology translator with Microsoft ecosystem adoption and solution enablement experience seeking a customer-facing cloud adoption or solution specialist bridge role.
positioning.headlineRecommendation: Business-to-Technology Translator | Microsoft 365 Copilot Adoption, Power Platform Automation & Enterprise AI Enablement
whyThisFits: Strong Microsoft ecosystem experience across Power BI, Power Apps, Power Automate, SharePoint, Microsoft 365 Copilot, and Copilot Studio.
Evidence of translating operational requirements into automation, analytics, governance, and AI adoption solutions.
Evidence of vendor coordination, customer-facing technical project execution, and stakeholder enablement.
hiddenSkillsToSurface: Microsoft 365 Copilot adoption analytics and license-conversion support
Power Platform solution evaluation and governance
Business-case-adjacent value framing through usage, adoption, licensing, and operational visibility
Technical handoff, deployment support, troubleshooting, and validation checklists
evidenceToSuppress: evi-global-intune-rollout: Prompt Context Only and too weak for cloud-sales positioning.
evi-n8n-saas-workflow-automation-exposure: Interview Only and unsupported as professional production experience.
evi-existing-python-sql-pipeline-debugging: use only as technical-operations context, not cloud engineering depth.
evi-trender-buddy-gpt-eval-pipeline: avoid presenting as Azure infrastructure or enterprise cloud sales ownership.
claimsToAvoid: Azure architecture or production cloud infrastructure ownership
interviewRiskQuestions: What sales quota did you carry and what percentage did you achieve?
Describe a multi-million-dollar Azure or cloud deal you closed.
How did you build and defend a customer business case?
Which executive stakeholders did you influence?
What Azure migration or application modernization engagements did you lead?
How did you co-sell with partners or ISVs?
How did you forecast pipeline and manage deal risk?

managerIntent.actualJobToBeDone: Acquire and close large enterprise Azure opportunities while coordinating Microsoft and partner resources to increase cloud consumption.
hiringManagerPainPoints: Deals stalled by technical, licensing, legal, or deployment blockers
Failure to convert technical interest into consumption and revenue
dealBreakers: Only technical or internal delivery experience
overqualificationRisks: Candidate may appear technically capable but commercially unproven.
Business process and HR technology specialization may seem unrelated to Azure enterprise sales.
AI and automation breadth could obscure the absence of quota and cloud-deal evidence.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: 7+ years technology sales or account management experience
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Client-facing technical project coordination and vendor alignment
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Evidence shows project execution and a sales specialist title but not 7+ years of quota-carrying sales or account management.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Leading large cloud engagements for enterprise customers
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Ownership of major cloud migration or modernization programs
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Technical implementation coordination and deployment participation
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No evidence of leading Azure, infrastructure migration, application modernization, or large global cloud engagements.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Executive relationship building and customer buy-in
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: C-level selling and executive sponsorship creation
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Produced adoption and licensing evidence for managers, leaders, Finance, Microsoft, and regional stakeholders
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Stakeholder coordination is supported, but executive relationship ownership and commercial buy-in are not.

jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Joint account planning and partner-led opportunity execution
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Vendor coordination and Microsoft-related program collaboration
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Vendor coordination is not equivalent to partner co-selling or ISV opportunity ownership.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Business cases, proposals, negotiation, and deal closure
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Supported client requirements, bidding, negotiation coordination, and delivery documentation
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Source supports coordination around bidding and negotiation but not proposal ownership, revenue, contract closure, or competitive displacement.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Pipeline management, forecasting, and quota attainment
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: CRM hygiene, forecast accuracy, and measurable sales performance
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No evidence of sales pipeline, forecast, quota, or cloud-consumption ownership.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: AI and digital transformation customer advisory
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Ability to connect AI and cloud solutions to customer business outcomes
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Enterprise AI adoption, governance, automation, analytics, and license-value enablement
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Strong solution translation evidence, but primarily internal rather than external customer advisory.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Remove deployment roadblocks and drive customer satisfaction
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Post-sale escalation leadership and customer-success ownership
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Production troubleshooting, operational handoff, issue resolution, and vendor coordination
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Operational support is supported; customer satisfaction ownership and cloud deployment escalation are not.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Azure, IaaS, PaaS, migration, and modernization knowledge
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Technical fluency sufficient for cloud solution selling
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Azure Data Explorer, Microsoft ecosystem, API integration, and deployment-support exposure
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Evidence does not establish Azure platform, IaaS/PaaS, cloud economics, migration, or modernization depth.

internalTerminology.originalTerm: M365 Copilot Trial GO Plus Program
internalTerminology.externalFriendlyWording: Enterprise Microsoft 365 Copilot adoption and license-conversion program
internalTerminology.reason: The internal program label is less useful than the business outcome and Microsoft product context.
Internal product name lacks external context.

internalTerminology.originalTerm: TOMO Consumer Chatbot Evaluation & A/B Testing
internalTerminology.externalFriendlyWording: Customer-service chatbot platform evaluation and A/B testing
internalTerminology.reason: Internal platform name should be translated into evaluation work and decision value.

internalTerminology.originalTerm: Legal DPIA App / AI Impact Assessment
internalTerminology.externalFriendlyWording: Automated privacy and AI-impact assessment workflow
internalTerminology.reason: Describe the business process rather than the internal application label.
HERO is an internal legacy-system name and may not be recognizable externally.

internalTerminology.externalFriendlyWording: Internal API engagement and integration governance model
internalTerminology.reason: Internal platform name is not a recognized public product in this context.
Personal name and internal handover label are not externally meaningful.

remainingGaps.gap: Quota-carrying enterprise technology sales and Azure consumption ownership
remainingGaps.mitigation: Do not claim it; pursue cloud adoption, technical account, solution enablement, or partner technical specialist roles where delivery evidence is relevant.

remainingGaps.gap: Large Azure cloud migration and application modernization engagements
remainingGaps.mitigation: Add only verified migration or modernization project evidence; otherwise position related Workday, Intune, API, and enterprise automation work as implementation support.

remainingGaps.gap: Executive selling, negotiation, proposal ownership, and deal closure
remainingGaps.mitigation: Use client-facing technical coordination evidence conservatively and prepare concrete examples of stakeholder influence, requirements discovery, and vendor negotiation.

remainingGaps.gap: Pipeline management, forecasting, and quota performance
remainingGaps.mitigation: Do not include sales metrics without source evidence; treat this JD as a stretch or avoid application.
Surface Azure Data Explorer and Microsoft ecosystem exposure only as supporting knowledge; obtain verified Azure training or project evidence if pursuing cloud roles.

qualityTargets.weakness: Candidate evidence does not support Azure enterprise sales seniority.
qualityTargets.improvement: Target Azure adoption, technical account, solution enablement, or Power Platform specialist roles instead.

qualityTargets.weakness: Microsoft and automation keywords are present, but quota, Azure sales, migration, forecasting, and deal keywords are unsupported.
qualityTargets.improvement: Include only truthful Microsoft ecosystem and customer-facing solution keywords; do not force missing sales terms.

qualityTargets.weakness: Strong technical delivery and adoption evidence but weak commercial evidence.
qualityTargets.improvement: Lead with Copilot adoption, vendor coordination, solution evaluation, and deployment support.

qualityTargets.weakness: High risk if technical delivery is presented as cloud sales ownership.
qualityTargets.improvement: Keep claims conservative and explicitly separate internal delivery from enterprise sales.

qualityTargets.weakness: Likely unable to answer quota, deal closure, forecast, and Azure migration questions with grounded evidence.
qualityTargets.improvement: Avoid this role unless additional verified sales evidence exists.

coreScreeningThresholds: 7+ years quota-carrying technology sales or account management
Azure or public cloud solution fluency
hardRequirements: 7+ years technology sales or account management
Large enterprise migration or modernization experience
Virtual-team leadership across sales, technical, services, licensing, and legal resources

ambiguousSignals.jdPhrase: Create buy-in vision with key decision makers
ambiguousSignals.likelyScreeningMeaning: Evidence of executive discovery, business-value storytelling, and sponsorship creation.
ambiguousSignals.howToShowItInCv: Use verified examples of presenting adoption, licensing, governance, or operational value to leaders; do not call this executive selling without proof.

ambiguousSignals.jdPhrase: Deep business expertise and technical knowledge within enterprise sales
ambiguousSignals.likelyScreeningMeaning: Ability to connect technical architecture and commercial value during enterprise sales cycles.
ambiguousSignals.howToShowItInCv: Show business-process translation across Microsoft automation, AI adoption, analytics, and governance.

ambiguousSignals.likelyScreeningMeaning: Own escalations across technical, commercial, licensing, legal, and partner teams until customer implementation progresses.
ambiguousSignals.howToShowItInCv: Use production handover, troubleshooting, UAT resolution, and vendor coordination evidence.

ambiguousSignals.likelyScreeningMeaning: Retain customer trust and demonstrate post-sale outcomes.
ambiguousSignals.howToShowItInCv: Use customer-service chatbot operations and client-facing technical delivery only within documented scope.

ambiguousSignals.likelyScreeningMeaning: Win against incumbent platforms through differentiated business value.

summaryAngle: This is an enterprise Azure sales role, and the candidate does not meet its core commercial screening thresholds. The safest truthful angle is Microsoft ecosystem adoption and solution enablement across Copilot, Power Platform, AI governance, analytics, and operational deployment support, but the application should be treated as Avoid unless additional verified enterprise sales evidence exists.
recommendedNextAction: Do not submit this CV for the Azure Solution Specialist role without verified quota-carrying enterprise sales and Azure cloud engagement evidence; prioritize Microsoft ecosystem adoption, Power Platform, AI operations, technical account, or solution enablement roles.

transferableStrengths.strength: Enterprise AI adoption, governance, automation, analytics, and license-value enablement
Production troubleshooting, operational handoff, issue resolution, and vendor coordination

The safest truthful angle is Microsoft ecosystem adoption and solution enablement across Copilot, Power Platform, AI governance, analytics, and operational deployment support, but the application should be treated as Avoid unless additional verified enterprise sales evidence exists.

Strengthen wording only when supported by selected evidence.
Preserve unsupported requirements as gaps or risks, not visible strengths.
